game_info.title: Neon Hop
game_info.concept:
Bounce off tiles and make as many hops as you can in this mind-blowing arcade action!

App Store URL: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/hop/id1154436120

================================================================================
Full Description:
================================================================================

Bounce off tiles and make as many hops as you can in this mind-blowing arcade action!

Touch the screen and drag left or right to guide the ball from tile to tile. Don’t miss the path! Make insane combos and beat your friend's scores!

What is your best score?
game_info.description:
Welcome to NEON HOP, a rhythm-based arcade platformer. 
Your objective is to guide the bouncing ball along an infinite path of neon tiles suspended in the void.
The ball bounces automatically; you must control its lateral movement to ensure it lands safely on the tiles.
Collect glowing data fragments to increase your score and maintain your rhythm.
The game speeds up as you progress—stay focused and don't fall into the abyss!
game_info.controls:
- Arrow Left: Steer ball left
- Arrow Right: Steer ball right
- Space: Boost jump (while in air to hover slightly)
- Shift: Dash downward (slam)
- Enter: Start Game
- ESC: Pause / Resume
- R: Restart Game

TEST_1.test_description: Verifies the automated bot's ability to track and land on tiles indefinitely.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The bot calculates the X-position of the nearest upcoming tile and applies steering forces to align the ball's X-position with the target tile's center.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The ball should successfully land on at least 50 consecutive tiles without falling, demonstrating stable pathfinding logic.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the physics engine's boundary handling and collision stability under chaotic input.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The bot inputs random steering directions (Left/Right) every few frames, disregarding tile positions.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The game should eventually trigger a "Game Over" state when the ball misses a tile, confirming that the fail state is reachable and correctly handled without crashing.
